name: dht
import: import dht  # DO NOT write: from dht import DHT11
description: DHT11, DHT22 (AM2302), DHT21 (AM2301) temperature and humidity sensor driver. Built into MicroPython firmware on ESP32.
differences_from_cpython: This module does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific sensor driver.

classes.name: DHT11

constructor_params.name: pin
constructor_params.description: Data pin (any GPIO). Must support input and open-drain output.

methods.name: DHT11.measure
methods.description: Trigger a sensor reading. Blocks for ~20ms. MUST call before reading temperature/humidity.
methods.note: Acquires the pin exclusively during measurement. Raises OSError(ETIMEDOUT) on failure.

returns.description: Temperature in Celsius. Call measure() first.
Relative humidity in %. Call measure() first.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin
import dht
import time

sensor = dht.DHT11(Pin(4))
while True:
    try:
        sensor.measure()
        temp = sensor.temperature()
        hum = sensor.humidity()
        print(f'Temperature: {temp}°C  Humidity: {hum}%')
    except OSError as e:
        print('Sensor read failed:', e)
    time.sleep(2)

classes.name: DHT22

methods.name: DHT22.measure
methods.description: Trigger a sensor reading. Blocks for ~20ms. Raises OSError(ETIMEDOUT) on failure.

methods.name: DHT22.temperature
methods.note: Call measure() first, otherwise returns stale data.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin
import dht

sensor = dht.DHT22(Pin(15))
sensor.measure()
print('Temperature:', sensor.temperature())  # e.g., 23.5
print('Humidity:', sensor.humidity())          # e.g., 65.2

timing_requirements: Minimum 1 second between measure() calls for DHT11.
Minimum 2 seconds between measure() calls for DHT22 (slower sensor).
DHT22 can fail if read too frequently — always handle OSError.
Use try/except around measure() — the one-wire protocol is timing-sensitive.
wiring: VCC → 3.3V (some modules tolerate 5V but use 3.3V to be safe)
DATA → Any GPIO (connect 4.7kΩ - 10kΩ pull-up resistor to 3.3V)
Some DHT modules have built-in pull-up resistor on the PCB.
common_bugs: Forgetting to call measure() before temperature()/humidity() — returns stale data.
Calling measure() too fast — DHT22 needs 2s between reads; raises OSError(ETIMEDOUT).
DHT11 returns integers, not floats. Type checking code may need adjustment.
No pull-up resistor — sensor may read all zeros or fail every time.
ESP32 deep-sleep wakes can interfere with DHT timing — add a short delay (100ms) after wake.
esp32_notes: Works on any GPIO pin (unlike some other sensors that need specific pins).
DHT sensor is timing-critical. Avoid interrupts during measure().
In deep-sleep applications, call sensor.measure() AFTER wake, not before sleep.
